data.title: Whale Tutorial: AI-Powered SOPs & Onboarding Made Easy
data.excerpt: Learn how to use Whale, the AI SOP software, to document processes and onboard teams faster. Follow our step-by-step guide to boost productivity. Start for free!

$media.alt: Whale Tutorial: AI-Powered SOPs & Onboarding Made Easy

children.text: If you've ever felt the pain of a new hire drowning in scattered docs or a team misfiring because they missed a process update, you know the cost of disorganized knowledge. I've been there. Whale is the tool that finally clicked for me, and I'm excited to walk you through exactly how to harness it. This step-by-step tutorial will show you how to use Whale's AI-powered platform to document SOPs, build internal training, and get your team aligned with stunning speed.
*Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you make a purchase through one of these links, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*
Step 1: Getting Started & Setting Up Your Workspace
First, head to
Whale's website

and sign up for their "Free Forever" plan. It's surprisingly generous, giving you 5 members and a creator seat to explore. The onboarding process for the software itself is a masterclass in good design—it guides you through setting up your first "Space." Think of a Space as a departmental hub (e.g., "Marketing," "Sales Ops," "Engineering"). You can create different Spaces for different teams, keeping everything organized but accessible. Invite your first few team members right from the dashboard. The interface is clean and intuitive, so even your least tech-savvy colleague will feel comfortable. This initial setup takes less than 15 minutes, and you immediately see the value of having a single source of truth.
Step 2: Document Your First SOP with the AI Assistant

This is where Whale truly shines. Don't start from a blank page. Click "Create New" and select "SOP." You can start writing, but the real magic is the AI assistant. Describe the process in a few sentences, like "How to onboard a new client from contract signing to kick-off call." The AI will generate a detailed, step-by-step draft template for you. It structures it logically, adds common steps you might forget, and even suggests headings. You then edit, tweak, and enrich it with your specifics—adding screenshots, embedding Loom videos directly into a step, or linking to relevant software. I created a comprehensive "Customer Support Escalation" SOP in 30 minutes that would have taken a full afternoon in Google Docs. For a deep dive into this feature, check out our article on
what makes Whale's AI so effective
. The key is that it doesn't just store documents; it helps you create better, clearer ones from the start.
This link
is your gateway to trying this AI co-pilot for yourself.
Step 3: Create a Training Course or Quiz

Once your SOP is polished, it's time to ensure your team learns it. Whale lets you turn any SOP into an interactive training module. You can create a "Course" by grouping related SOPs together. For our client onboarding SOP, you might add it to a "New Hire Onboarding" course. Within a course, you can add quizzes at the end of each section. Whale provides templates for multiple-choice, true/false, and short-answer questions. This transforms passive reading into active learning. Imagine your new sales rep not just reading how to use the CRM, but being quizzed on key steps after each module. The platform tracks completion and quiz scores automatically. If you're looking for inspiration on structuring these, our guide on
Whale use cases
has fantastic examples for sales enablement, compliance training, and more.
Step 4: Assign, Track, and Manage Team Progress

Now, assign the course. Go to the training module, click "Assign," and select individuals or entire teams. Set due dates and Whale will send automatic reminders. This is a game-changer for compliance training or seasonal processes. The dashboard gives you a clear overview of who has completed what, their quiz scores, and who's falling behind. You can see engagement metrics at a glance. No more chasing people down via email. It’s a proactive system. The progress tracking isn’t just for managers; each employee has their own "My Training" view, showing what's assigned to them and their completion status. This fosters accountability and a sense of accomplishment. For teams already using project management tools, Whale’s
integrations
are robust, fitting into your existing workflow rather than replacing it.
Step 5: Leverage New Features & Integrations

Whale is constantly evolving. A recent standout is the even deeper AI integration, which can now suggest updates to your SOPs based on how your team uses them or highlight outdated sections. They’ve also expanded their template library significantly. You can start with a pre-built template for dozens of common business processes and customize it, saving hours. Integrations are key for adoption. Whale connects seamlessly with tools your team already lives in, like Slack (for notifications and quick searches), Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365. This means an update to a SOP in Whale can trigger a notification in a relevant Slack channel, keeping everyone in the loop without them having to leave their workflow. Stay updated on all the latest enhancements by reading our
new features roundup
. They also offer fantastic, practical advice in their
tips and best practices
section, which I highly recommend browsing.
Who Is Whale Ideal For? Finding Your Perfect Fit
Whale isn't just for enterprises. Its flexibility makes it perfect for:
Rapidly Scaling Startups:
When you're hiring fast, you can't afford to have new hires shadowing someone for a week. Whale systematizes tribal knowledge into scalable training, ensuring consistency from hire #10 to hire #100.
HR & People Operations Teams:
This is your powerhouse for formalizing onboarding programs, compliance training, and creating a consistent employee experience. It directly reduces the repetitive questions HR fields daily.
Operations & Process-Driven Roles:
If your work is about efficiency, Whale is your bible. It documents the "how" so you can focus on the "what's next." For operations teams, it's a non-negotiable tool for quality control.
Agencies & Professional Services:
Client onboarding and methodology training become streamlined. You can even create client-specific SOPs for transparent collaboration.
Hybrid & Remote Teams:
With knowledge centralized and accessible 24/7, team members in any time zone can find answers and complete training on their schedule, which is essential for distributed workforces.
Final Thoughts & Your Next Step

Whale fundamentally changed how my team shares and retains knowledge. The shift from static, forgotten documents to living, breathing, and interactive training is profound. The AI assistance removes the biggest barrier to starting (the blank page), and the tracking ensures it's not just created, but actually learned. The productivity gains are real—less time spent answering the same questions, faster ramp-up for new hires, and more consistent execution of key processes across the board.

You've seen the steps: from setup and AI-assisted SOP creation to building courses and tracking progress. The platform is designed for real-world teams dealing with real-world chaos. If you're ready to stop losing knowledge in email threads and start building a trainable, scalable foundation for your business, the best way to understand its power is to experience it.
Start your free Whale account today
and document your first SOP in the next 20 minutes. You'll feel the difference immediately.

children.text: Is Whale really free to start?

Yes, Whale offers a "Free Forever" plan that includes 5 team members and 1 creator. It's fully functional for exploring the platform, creating SOPs, and building initial training courses, making it a risk-free way to test its value for your team.
How does Whale's AI SOP assistant work?

The AI assistant helps you draft SOPs by generating a structured, step-by-step template based on a simple description of the process you provide. You then edit and customize this draft with your specific details, media, and links, dramatically speeding up documentation from scratch.
Can Whale integrate with our existing tools?

Absolutely. Whale integrates with popular platforms like Slack, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365. This allows for features like search-in-context from Slack and automated notifications, ensuring Whale fits into your team's existing workflow rather than disrupting it.
Is Whale suitable for a small business or startup?

Whale is exceptionally well-suited for small businesses and startups. Its scalable design and free starting tier allow you to systematize knowledge from the very beginning. As you grow, the platform grows with you, preventing the knowledge chaos that often accompanies rapid hiring.
